channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ERC allows flexible installment payments and pauses electricity disconnections from May to July 2026
item.description: The Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) has announced that residential and non-residential consumers who are unable to pay their electricity bills will not face disconnection from May to July 2026. The...

item.title: Man alleges unprovoked police assault at Sydney protest, plans legal action
item.description: Derek Jones, 56, known as “white shirt guy,” claims he was assaulted by police during a protest in Sydney against Israeli President Isaac Herzog in February. The incident, which left Jones with two br...

item.title: Arsenal qualify for the Champions League final after two decades
item.description: Arsenal Football Club has secured a place in the Champions League final for the first time in 20 years, marking a major milestone in the club's European campaign. This achievement highlights the team'...

item.title: Parliament Hill vigil calls for concrete action on missing and murdered Indigenous women and girls
item.description: A vigil held on Parliament Hill marked Red Dress Day, a national day of awareness for missing and murdered Indigenous women, girls and two-spirit people (MMIWG2S+), while amplifying calls for concrete...

item.title: Arsenal secure place in Champions League final after defeating Atletico Madrid
item.description: Arsenal have booked their spot in the Champions League final for only the second time in their history, following a tense 1-0 victory over Atletico Madrid at the Emirates Stadium. Bukayo Saka’s tap-in...

item.title: a16z Crypto Launches $2.2 Billion Fund Amid Slowing Market
item.description: Amid a cooling cryptocurrency market, venture capital firm a16z crypto announced the launch of its fifth fund, raising $2.2 billion and bringing its total assets under management to $9.8 billion. The ...

item.title: Arsenal Reach Champions League Final After 20-Year Wait
item.description: Arsenal don finally reach Champions League final after 20 years as Bukayo Saka score the only goal wey settle the semi-final against Atletico Madrid. The Gunners hold firm after the 1-0 win for Emirat...

item.title: BJP's Strategy and Suvendu Adhikari Lead to Mamata Banerjee’s Defeat in Bhabanipur
item.description: In a significant political development, Mamata Banerjee, the Chief Minister of West Bengal, faced a shocking defeat in her stronghold of Bhabanipur. The BJP, under the active guidance of Union Home Mi...

item.title: EU urges U.S. to uphold existing tariff agreement amid threats of car duty increases
item.description: The European Union has called on the United States to honour their 2025 tariff agreement, following threats from former U.S. President Donald Trump to increase duties on European cars. The pact, agree...

item.title: Government Approves Rs 18,100 Crore ECLGS 5.0 for MSMEs and Airlines Affected by West Asia Conflict
item.description: The Indian government has launched the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme (ECLGS) 5.0 with an outlay of Rs 18,100 crore to support MSMEs and airlines facing financial stress due to the ongoing Wes...

item.title: Treasurer cites Middle East conflict as factor in RBA interest rate rise; Inland Rail funding cancelled
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has raised interest rates by 0.25 percentage points to 4.35%, marking the third consecutive hike and returning rates to levels seen before last year’s brief cuts. T...

item.title: Kevin Kiarie Overcomes Federation Hurdle with Friend's Support to Win Two Golds in Africa
item.description: Kenya's skating star Kevin Kiarie Ruhiu almost missed the 2026 African Skating Championships in Cairo because his federation had not settled the affiliation fees with World Skate. Upon arrival, he dis...

item.title: INEC Announces Final Phase of Voter Registration Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don announce say dem go resume the last phase of the Continuous Voter Registration (CVR) across Nigeria for the 2027 general elections. According t...

item.title: Department of Labour reports R300m discrepancies due to Treasury system upgrade
item.description: The South African Minister of Employment and Labour, Nomakhosazana Meth, has attributed discrepancies amounting to over R300 million in her department’s accounts to issues with the National Treasury’s...

item.title: High Court civil trial against Andrew Tate postponed amid police reinvestigation
item.description: The civil trial against former professional kickboxer and influencer Andrew Tate has been adjourned in the High Court. This follows the reopening of a police investigation into sexual abuse allegation...

item.title: Mother of Deceased Kolkata Doctor Wins Panihati Assembly Seat Representing BJP
item.description: In a significant development in West Bengal politics, Ratna Debnath, mother of the late RG Kar trainee doctor who was tragically raped and murdered, has won the Panihati constituency in the state asse...

item.title: Tanzania's Digital Infrastructure Puts It at the Forefront of Africa’s Tech Growth
item.description: Tanzania is rapidly positioning itself as a key player in Africa’s growing digital economy. With the launch of Dira 2050, the country aims for a one-trillion-dollar economy by 2050, heavily relying on...

item.title: Fuel Prices Rise Amid Fastest Inflation in Three Years
item.description: Fuel prices in the Philippines increased again on May 5, 2026, as inflation accelerated to 7.2% in April, the fastest pace seen in three years. The Department of Energy (DOE) reported that diesel pric...

item.title: Federal Government Plans Partial Sale of Canadian Photonics Fabrication Centre to Boost Investment
item.description: The Canadian government, through the National Research Council, announced plans to sell a stake in the Canadian Photonics Fabrication Centre (CPFC) to attract private and foreign investment while reta...

item.title: Trump Suspends Project to Escort Ships Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Diplomatic Progress with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced that the United States will temporarily pause its initiative, known as Project Freedom, intended to escort foreign vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. The decisi...
